trivia_bullets.text: Run Away Together was first printed in Throne of Eldraine (October 2019, no. 62/269), where it was illustrated by Filip Burburan — a Croatian painter known for traditional acrylic and oil work. As of Scryfall's records, this CMR printing is its third appearance; the card has since been reprinted nine times across Throne of Eldraine, Commander Legends, Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate, Wilds of Eldraine Commander, Game Night: Free-for-All, Bloomburrow, The List (CLB-92), Foundations, and Lorwyn Eclipsed (2026). [Scryfall: prints search for Run Away Together oracle ID]

"Virtue is virtue, whatever the heart that nurtures it." — Malacan, Vantress exile. The speaker, Malacan, is identified as a Vantress exile — a figure from the school of mind-magic on Eldraine, suggesting a scholar cast out for unorthodox views. The line frames the card's art (an improbable monster-and-mage duo running together) as a meditation on moral universalism. [Scryfall: flavor_text field]
sources: Scryfall API: flavor_text field for card d747d5f2-d3b0-44f9-92f0-781839386850

trivia_bullets.text: Run Away Together requires two targets controlled by different players — a design constraint that creates unique gameplay tension in multiplayer Commander: the caster must always return one of their own creatures alongside an opponent's. This is a deliberate asymmetry forcing diplomatic card usage, which is why it was considered one of the strongest commons in Commander Legends limited. EDHREC ranks it at 1704 among all cards. [Scryfall: oracle_text; Scryfall: edhrec_rank; dotesports.com: 'Throne of Eldraine brings the feels with Run Away Together', via WebSearch snippet]
sources: dotesports.com: Throne of Eldraine Run Away Together preview — WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Artist-prized commons signal: Filip Burburan's original MTG paintings have sold at auction for $4,450 to $9,000 each (Xyris, the Writhing Storm sold for $9,000; Brokkos, Apex of Forever for $5,600; Akim, the Soaring Wind for $4,450 — all from Ikoria, 2020). Burburan works in traditional acrylic and oil on board. His original art market value is among the highest for active Magic illustrators. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Three Filip Burburan Legends Fetch Nearly $20,000 Combined' (2020); Hipsters of the Coast: 'Filip Buruban's Nyxbloom Ancient Sells for $7,500']
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Three Filip Burburan Legends Fetch Nearly $20,000 Combined' (May 2020)
Hipsters of the Coast: 'Filip Buruban's Nyxbloom Ancient Sells for $7,500'
